rules.rule: Excl: the patient has predominantly squamous histology non small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received solid organ or blood system transplantation**descriptive
Incl: the patient has ast and alt levels ≤ 5 × uln if with documented liver metastases**descriptive
Excl: the patient has clinically active diverticulitis, intra abdominal abscess, or gastrointestinal obstruction**descriptive
Incl: max age (years)**75.0
Incl: the patient has adequate renal function, defined as serum creatinine ≤ 1.5 × uln or calculated creatinine clearance ≥ 60 ml/min (cockcroft gault formula)**descriptive
Incl: the patient has urine protein < 2+ or 24 hour urine protein < 1 g**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not received prior systemic antineoplastic therapy for advanced or recurrent non squamous nsclc**descriptive
Incl: the patient has aspartate aminotransferase (ast) and alanine aminotransferase (alt) levels ≤ 2.5 × uln if without liver metastases**descriptive
Incl: min absolute neutrophil count (10^9/l)**1.5
Incl: min hemoglobin (g/l)**90.0
Incl: min platelet count (10^9/l)**100.0
Incl: the patient has adequate hepatic function, defined as total bilirubin level ≤ 1.5 × upper limit of normal (uln)**descriptive
Incl: the patient has adequate coagulation function, defined as international normalized ratio (inr) or prothrombin time (pt) ≤ 1.5 × uln**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a known history of human immunodeficiency virus (hiv) infection (hiv 1/2 antibodies positive), known active syphilis infection, or active pulmonary tuberculosis**descriptive

Excl: the patient has uncontrolled concurrent medical conditions such as esophageal or gastric varices requiring immediate intervention or with a high risk of bleeding, hepatic encephalopathy, hepatorenal syndrome, child pugh b or worse cirrhosis, significant malnutrition (e.g., need for intravenous nutrition supplements), tumor compressing surrounding vital organs with relevant symptoms, class ii iv congestive heart failure (new york heart association classification), poorly controlled and clinically significant arrhythmia, uncontrolled arterial hypertension (systolic blood pressure 160 mmhg or higher or diastolic blood pressure 100 mmhg or higher) even after standard treatment, any arterial thrombosis, embolism, or ischemia such as myocardial infarction, unstable angina, cerebrovascular accident, or transient ischemic attack within 6 months prior to enrollment, or a history of deep vein thrombosis, pulmonary embolism, or any other serious thromboembolism within 3 months prior to enrollment (thrombosis caused by implantable venous port or catheter, or superficial venous thrombosis is not considered serious thromboembolism)**descriptive

Excl: the patient has other malignancies diagnosed within 5 years prior to the first dose of study treatment, except for radically treated basal cell carcinoma of the skin, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, and/or radically resected carcinoma in situ**descriptive
Excl: the patient has active hepatitis c virus (hcv) infection (hcv antibody positive and hcv rna level above the lower limit of detection)**descriptive
Incl: the female patient of childbearing potential has a negative urine or serum pregnancy test within 3 days prior to receiving the first dose of study drug**descriptive

Excl: the patient has known untreated active hepatitis b (patients with hepatitis b are eligible if hbv viral load is less than 1000 copies/ml (200 iu/ml) or below the lower limit of detection before the first dose of study treatment and should receive anti hbv therapy to avoid viral reactivation throughout the study treatment; for patients with anti hbc positive, hbsag negative, anti hbs negative, and hbv viral load negative, prophylactic anti hbv treatment is not required, but viral reactivation should be closely monitored)**descriptive
Incl: the female patient with a positive or indeterminate urine pregnancy test must have a negative serum pregnancy test before receiving the first dose of study drug**descriptive

Excl: the patient has received a live vaccine within 30 days prior to the first dose of study treatment (inactivated viral vaccines for seasonal influenza within 30 days prior to the first dose are allowed; live attenuated influenza vaccines administered intranasally are not allowed)**descriptive

Excl: the patient has a known history of non infectious pneumonitis requiring corticosteroid therapy within 1 year prior to the first dose of study treatment or has current interstitial lung disease**descriptive
Incl: the patient receiving anticoagulant therapy is eligible if the pt is within the proposed range for the anticoagulant**descriptive
Incl: the patient has adequate hematopoietic function without transfusion in 7 days and without dependence on erythropoietin (epo)**descriptive

Excl: the patient has active autoimmune disease that has required systemic therapy (such as disease modifying agents, corticosteroids, or immunosuppressive drugs) within 2 years prior to the first dose of study treatment**descriptive
Excl: the patient has active infection requiring systemic therapy**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received radiotherapy in the lung with a dose greater than 30 gy within 6 months prior to the first dose of study treatment**descriptive

Excl: the patient has not adequately recovered from toxicity and/or complications caused by any intervention prior to starting study treatment (i.e., toxicity greater than grade 1 or not returned to baseline, excluding asthenia or alopecia)**descriptive

Excl: the patient has clinically uncontrollable pleural effusion or ascites (patients who do not need drainage of effusion or have no significant increase in effusion 3 days after stopping drainage can be enrolled)**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a psychiatric or substance abuse disorder that would interfere with compliance with the requirements of the study**descriptive
Excl: the patient has completed palliative radiotherapy within 7 days prior to the first dose of study treatment**descriptive

Excl: the patient is diagnosed with immunodeficiency or is receiving systemic glucocorticoid therapy or any other form of immunosuppressive therapy within 7 days prior to the first dose of study treatment (physiological doses of glucocorticoids, defined as 10 mg/day or less of prednisone or equivalent, are allowed)**descriptive

Incl: the patient has a histologically or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of locally advanced (stage iiib/iiic), metastatic, or recurrent (stage iv) nonsquamous non small cell lung cancer (nsclc) according to the 8th edition of the tnm classification**descriptive

Excl: the patient has received chinese medicines with anti lung cancer indications or immunomodulatory drugs (including thymosin, interferon, interleukin, except for local use to control pleural effusion) within 2 weeks prior to the first dose of drug treatment**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a known severe allergic reaction (grade 3 or higher) to any component of sintilimab, pemetrexed, cisplatin, carboplatin, or any excipients**descriptive
Excl: the patient with mixed cell types must have at least 90% adenocarcinoma cells**descriptive

Excl: the patient is currently participating in an interventional clinical study or has received other investigational drugs or devices within 4 weeks prior to the first dose of drug treatment**descriptive
Excl: the patient has undergone major surgery within 3 weeks prior to the first dose of drug treatment**descriptive

Excl: the patient has a history of idiopathic pulmonary fibrosis, organizing pneumonia (e.g., bronchiolitis obliterans), drug-induced pneumonitis, idiopathic pneumonitis, or evidence of active pneumonitis on screening chest ct scan**descriptive
Excl: the patient has had a prior allogeneic bone marrow transplantation or solid organ transplant**descriptive

Excl: the patient has any other disease, metabolic dysfunction, physical examination finding, or clinical laboratory finding giving reasonable suspicion of a disease or condition that contraindicates the use of an investigational drug or may affect the interpretation of the results or render the patient at high risk for treatment complications**descriptive
Incl: min absolute neutrophil count (cells/µl)**1500.0
Excl: the patient has uncontrolled pleural effusion, pericardial effusion, or ascites requiring recurrent drainage procedures once monthly or more frequently**descriptive
Incl: min lymphocyte count (/µl)**500.0

Excl: the patient has a history of autoimmune disease, including but not limited to myasthenia gravis, myositis, autoimmune hepatitis, systemic lupus erythematosus, rheumatoid arthritis, inflammatory bowel disease, vascular thrombosis associated with antiphospholipid syndrome, wegener’s granulomatosis, sjögren’s syndrome, guillain-barré syndrome, multiple sclerosis, vasculitis, or glomerulonephritis**descriptive
Incl: min platelet count (/µl)**100000.0

Incl: the patient has a serum bilirubin level of no more than 1.25 times the upper limit of normal (uln), except for patients with known gilbert disease who have a serum bilirubin level of no more than 3 times the uln**descriptive
Incl: the patient may be transfused to meet this criterion**descriptive
Incl: min hemoglobin level (g/dl)**9.0
Excl: active hepatitis c virus infection**present

Excl: the patient has significant cardiovascular disease, such as new york heart association cardiac disease class ii or greater, myocardial infarction, or cerebrovascular accident within 3 months prior to randomization, unstable arrhythmias, or unstable angina**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a left ventricular ejection fraction less than 50% and is not on a stable, optimized medical regimen**descriptive

Incl: the patient has ast, alt, and alkaline phosphatase levels of no more than 2.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln), except for patients with documented liver metastases (ast and/or alt ≤ 5 x uln) and patients with documented liver or bone metastases (alkaline phosphatase ≤ 5 x uln)**descriptive

Excl: the patient has received a live, attenuated influenza vaccine (e.g., flumist) within 4 weeks prior to randomization, during treatment, or within 5 months following the last dose of atezolizumab/placebo**descriptive
Incl: the patient has an inr or aptt of no more than 1.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln), unless receiving therapeutic anticoagulation and is on a stable dose**descriptive

Excl: the patient has a malignancy other than small cell lung cancer (sclc) within 5 years prior to randomization, except for those with negligible risk of metastasis or death treated with expected curative outcome**descriptive

Excl: the patient has spinal cord compression not definitively treated with surgery and/or radiation, or previously diagnosed and treated spinal cord compression without evidence that disease has been clinically stable for at least 1 week prior to randomization**descriptive
Excl: the patient has eczema, psoriasis, lichen simplex chronicus, or vitiligo that is not well controlled at baseline or requires more than low potency topical steroids**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a history of severe allergic, anaphylactic, or other hypersensitivity reactions to chimeric or humanized antibodies or fusion proteins**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a serum creatinine level of no more than 1.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln)**descriptive

Excl: the patient has received treatment with systemic immunosuppressive medications (including but not limited to corticosteroids, cyclophosphamide, azathioprine, methotrexate, thalidomide, and anti-tumor necrosis factor agents) within 1 week prior to randomization**descriptive
Excl: the patient has had prior treatment with cd137 agonists or immune checkpoint blockade therapies, anti–pd-1, or anti–pd-l1 therapeutic antibodies**descriptive
Excl: the patient is pregnant, lactating, or intending to become pregnant during the study**descriptive
Excl: the patient has severe infections at the time of randomization, including but not limited to hospitalization for complications of infection, bacteremia, or severe pneumonia**descriptive

Incl: the patient has histologically or cytologically confirmed extensive stage small cell lung cancer (es sclc) according to the veterans administration lung study group (valg) staging system**descriptive

Incl: the female patient of childbearing potential agrees to remain abstinent or use contraceptive methods with a failure rate of less than 1% per year during the treatment period and for at least 5 months after the last dose of study treatment**descriptive
Excl: active hepatitis b**present
Excl: the patient has illnesses or conditions that interfere with their capacity to understand, follow, and/or comply with study procedures**descriptive

Incl: the patient must submit a pre treatment tumor tissue sample, which can be any available tumor tissue sample, before or within 4 weeks after randomization; however, enrollment can occur before the sample is submitted**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a history of allergic reactions to carboplatin or etoposide**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not received prior systemic treatment for es sclc**descriptive

Incl: the female patient is considered of childbearing potential if she is postmenarcheal, has not reached a postmenopausal state (defined as at least 12 continuous months of amenorrhea with no identified cause other than menopause), and has not undergone surgical sterilization (removal of ovaries and/or uterus)**descriptive
Excl: the patient has active or untreated central nervous system (cns) metastases as determined by ct or mri evaluation during screening and prior radiographic assessments**descriptive
Excl: active tuberculosis**present
Incl: the patient does not use periodic abstinence (e.g., calendar, ovulation, symptothermal, or postovulation methods) or withdrawal as acceptable methods of contraception**descriptive
Excl: the patient has uncontrolled or symptomatic hypercalcemia**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have any history of organ transplant that requires use of immunosuppressives**descriptive
Incl: min hemoglobin (g/dl)**9.0
Incl: min platelet count (/mcl)**100000.0

Incl: the patient has either alanine aminotransferase (alt) or aspartate aminotransferase (ast) less than or equal to 2 times the iuln within 28 days prior to re-treatment registration; for patients with liver metastases, either alt or ast must be less than or equal to 5 times the iuln**descriptive
Incl: min absolute neutrophil count (/mcl)**1500.0

Incl: the patient is not planning to receive any concurrent chemotherapy, immunotherapy, biologic, or hormonal therapy for cancer treatment; concurrent use of hormones for non-cancer-related conditions is acceptable**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have any history of primary immunodeficiency**descriptive

Incl: the patient has a serum bilirubin less than or equal to the institutional upper limit of normal (iuln) within 28 days prior to re-treatment registration; for patients with liver metastases, bilirubin must be less than or equal to 5 times the iuln**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have a known history of tuberculosis**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a serum creatinine less than or equal to the iuln or a measured or calculated creatinine clearance of at least 50 ml/min using the cockcroft-gault formula**descriptive
Incl: the patient whose only measurable disease is within a previous radiation therapy port must demonstrate clearly progressive disease prior to re-treatment registration**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have known human immunodeficiency virus (hiv), hepatitis b, or hepatitis c positivity**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have known hiv, hepatitis b, or hepatitis c positivity**descriptive
Incl: pregnancy status**no
Incl: the patient has a prestudy history and physical exam obtained within 28 days prior to re-treatment registration**descriptive
Incl: phase ii/iii biomarker-driven master protocol for previously treated squamous cell lung cancer (lung-map)**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have vitiligo, alopecia, grave's disease, or psoriasis requiring systemic treatment within the past 3 years**descriptive

Incl: the patient has not received any immunosuppressive medication within 28 days prior to sub-study registration and is not planning to receive any such agents while on protocol treatment, except for intranasal and inhaled corticosteroids or systemic corticosteroids at physiologic doses not to exceed 10 mg/day of prednisone or equivalent**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a zubrod performance status of 0-1 documented within 28 days prior to re-treatment registration**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not had any prior exposure to immunotherapy such as anti-programmed death 1 (pd-1) or anti-pd-l1 antibodies**descriptive

Incl: the patient has not received any immunosuppressive medication within 28 days prior to re-treatment registration and is not planning to receive any such agents while on protocol treatment, except for intranasal and inhaled corticosteroids or systemic corticosteroids at physiologic doses not to exceed 10 mg/day of prednisone or equivalent**descriptive

Incl: the patient has not received any treatment after discontinuing medi4736 except for localized palliative radiation therapy completed at least 14 days prior to re-treatment registration or local treatment for brain metastases**descriptive
Incl: the patient may have measurable or non-measurable disease documented by computed tomography (ct) or magnetic resonance imaging (mri)**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have any known allergy or reaction to any component of the medi4736 formulation**descriptive
Incl: smoking status**yes
Incl: min smoking history (pack years)**20.0
Incl: max age (years)**80.0
Incl: the patient has no previous history of cancer in the 10 years before the beginning of the study**descriptive
Excl: the patient has predominantly squamous cell histology non small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive

Excl: the patient is unable to interrupt aspirin or other nonsteroidal anti inflammatory drugs (nsaids), other than an aspirin dose less than or equal to 1.3 g per day, for a 5 day period (8 day period for long acting agents such as piroxicam)**descriptive
Incl: min creatinine clearance (ml/min)**50.0
Incl: the patient has a performance status of 0 or 1 on the eastern cooperative oncology group (ecog) performance status scale**descriptive
Excl: gastrointestinal obstruction**present
Excl: the patient has symptomatic ascites or pleural effusion, unless clinically stable following treatment for these conditions**descriptive
Incl: min absolute neutrophil count (/mcl)**1500.0

Incl: the patient has a serum total bilirubin level of no more than 1.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln), or direct bilirubin within uln for subjects with total bilirubin levels greater than 1.5 times uln**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a hemoglobin level of at least 9.0 g/dl or at least 5.6 mmol/l, without transfusions for 4 weeks**descriptive
Excl: intra abdominal abscess**present
Incl: the patient has measurable disease based on recist 1.1 as determined by the local site investigator or radiology assessment**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received prior systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy for metastatic disease before the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Incl: min platelet count (/mcl)**100000.0
Excl: diverticulitis**present

Excl: the patient has known active hepatitis c, defined by a known positive hep c antibody result and known quantitative hcv rna results greater than the lower limits of detection of the assay**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a history of non infectious pneumonitis that required steroids or current pneumonitis**descriptive
Incl: the patient has target lesions situated in a previously irradiated area that are considered measurable if progression has been demonstrated in such lesions**descriptive
Excl: the patient has active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not received prior systemic treatment for advanced or metastatic nsclc**descriptive
Excl: the patient received radiation therapy to the lung at a dose greater than 30 gy within 6 months of the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received antineoplastic biological therapy for metastatic disease before the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Excl: the patient had prior treatment with any other anti pd 1, pd l1, or pd l2 agent or an antibody targeting other immuno regulatory receptors or mechanisms**descriptive
Excl: human immunodeficiency virus**present
Excl: the patient has an active infection requiring therapy**descriptive
Excl: the patient is pregnant or breastfeeding, or expecting to conceive or father children within the projected duration of the study**descriptive
Excl: hepatitis b**present

Incl: the patient who received adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy is eligible if the adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy was completed at least 12 months prior to the development of metastatic disease**descriptive
Incl: min life expectancy (months)**3.0
Incl: the patient may provide biopsies obtained prior to receipt of adjuvant or neoadjuvant chemotherapy if a recent biopsy is not feasible**descriptive

Excl: the patient has a known history of prior malignancy except if the patient has undergone potentially curative therapy with no evidence of disease recurrence for 5 years since initiation of that therapy, with exceptions for certain in situ cancers and skin cancers**descriptive
Excl: the patient completed palliative radiotherapy within 7 days of the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive

Incl: the patient has provided tumor tissue from locations not radiated prior to biopsy, with formalin-fixed specimens after diagnosis of metastatic disease preferred for pd-l1 status determination prior to randomization**descriptive
Excl: the patient previously had a severe hypersensitivity reaction to treatment with another monoclonal antibody**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a mixed tumor categorized by the predominant cell type, but is ineligible if small cell elements are present**descriptive
Incl: the patient has ast (sgot) and alt (sgpt) levels no more than 2.5 times uln, or no more than 5 times uln for subjects with liver metastases**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a histologically-confirmed or cytologically-confirmed diagnosis of stage iv (m1a or m1b, ajcc 7th edition) nonsquamous non-small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive
Excl: peritoneal carcinomatosis**present

Excl: the patient is, at the time of signing informed consent, a known regular user of any illicit drugs or had a recent history (within the last year) of substance abuse including alcohol**descriptive
Incl: the patient has an international normalized ratio (inr) or prothrombin time (pt) of no more than 1.5 times uln unless receiving anticoagulant therapy**descriptive

Excl: the patient has known active central nervous system (cns) metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis, unless previously treated brain metastases are clinically stable for at least 2 weeks, with no evidence of new or enlarging brain metastases, and the patient is off steroids for 3 days prior to dosing**descriptive
Excl: the patient is unable or unwilling to take folic acid or vitamin b12 supplementation**descriptive
Excl: the patient has unknown or indeterminate alk status**descriptive
Incl: the patient may have received prior adjuvant or neoadjuvant chemotherapy for early stage lung cancer if all toxicities have resolved or stabilized**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not received prior systemic therapy for advanced or metastatic nsclc, except for one or two prior lines of first or second generation egfr tki and or osimertinib**descriptive
Excl: small cell lung cancer transformation**present
Incl: the patient’s tissue sample must be a core needle biopsy, excisional, or incisional biopsy**descriptive

Incl: the patient may have target lesions located in a previously irradiated field if there is documented radiographic disease progression in that site after completion of radiation therapy**descriptive
Excl: the patient has predominantly squamous cell histology non small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive

Excl: the patient is unable to interrupt aspirin or other nonsteroidal anti inflammatory drugs (nsaids), other than an aspirin dose less than or equal to 1.3 g per day, for a 5 day period (8 day period for long acting agents such as piroxicam)**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a histologically-confirmed or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of stage iv (m1a or m1b, ajcc 7th edition) nonsquamous non-small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive
Excl: gastrointestinal obstruction**present

Incl: min hemoglobin (g/dl)**9.0
Excl: the patient has symptomatic ascites or pleural effusion, unless clinically stable following treatment for these conditions**descriptive
Incl: min hemoglobin (mmol/l)**5.6
Incl: min platelet count (/mcl)**100000.0
Excl: the patient has known active central nervous system (cns) metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis**descriptive
Incl: the patient may provide biopsies obtained prior to receipt of adjuvant or neoadjuvant chemotherapy if a recent biopsy is not feasible**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a performance status of 0 or 1 on the eastern cooperative oncology group (ecog) performance status**descriptive

Incl: the patient has a serum total bilirubin of no more than 1.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln), or direct bilirubin within uln for subjects with total bilirubin levels greater than 1.5 times uln**descriptive
Excl: intra abdominal abscess**present
Excl: the patient has received prior systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy for metastatic disease before the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Excl: diverticulitis**present

Excl: the patient has a history of (non infectious) pneumonitis that required steroids or current pneumonitis**descriptive

Incl: the patient has confirmation that egfr or alk-directed therapy is not indicated, as evidenced by documentation of absence of tumor activating egfr mutations and absence of alk gene rearrangements or presence of a k-ras mutation**descriptive

Incl: the patient has provided tumor tissue from locations not radiated prior to biopsy, with formalin-fixed specimens after diagnosis of metastatic disease preferred for determination of pd-l1 status prior to randomization**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received antineoplastic biological therapy (e.g., erlotinib, crizotinib, cetuximab) for metastatic disease before the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Excl: the patient has active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years (with disease modifying agents, corticosteroids, or immunosuppressive drugs)**descriptive
Incl: the patient has target lesions situated in a previously irradiated area that are considered measurable if progression has been demonstrated in such lesions**descriptive
Excl: the patient received radiation therapy to the lung at a dose greater than 30 gy within 6 months of the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not received prior systemic treatment for advanced or metastatic nsclc**descriptive
Incl: the patient has measurable disease based on recist 1.1 as determined by the local site investigator or radiology assessment**descriptive
Incl: min life expectancy (months)**3.0
Excl: the patient had prior treatment with any other anti pd 1, pd l1, or pd l2 agent or an antibody targeting other immuno regulatory receptors or mechanisms**descriptive
Excl: human immunodeficiency virus**present
Excl: the patient has an active infection requiring therapy**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a mixed tumor categorized by the predominant cell type**descriptive
Excl: the patient is pregnant or breastfeeding, or expecting to conceive or father children within the projected duration of the study**descriptive
Excl: hepatitis b**present

Excl: the patient with previously treated brain metastases may participate if clinically stable for at least 2 weeks, with no evidence of new or enlarging brain metastases, and off steroids 3 days prior to dosing**descriptive

Excl: the patient has a known history of prior malignancy except if the patient has undergone potentially curative therapy with no evidence of disease recurrence for 5 years since initiation of that therapy, with exceptions for certain in situ cancers and skin cancers**descriptive

Incl: the patient who received adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy is eligible if the adjuvant or neoadjuvant therapy was completed at least 12 months prior to the development of metastatic disease**descriptive
Excl: the patient completed palliative radiotherapy within 7 days of the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Incl: min creatinine clearance (ml/min)**50.0
Excl: the patient previously had a severe hypersensitivity reaction to treatment with another monoclonal antibody**descriptive
Excl: the patient is on chronic systemic steroids**descriptive
Incl: the patient has ast (sgot) and alt (sgpt) levels no more than 2.5 times uln, or no more than 5 times uln for subjects with liver metastases**descriptive
Excl: peritoneal carcinomatosis**present

Excl: the patient is, at the time of signing informed consent, a known regular user of any illicit drugs or had a recent history (within the last year) of substance abuse including alcohol**descriptive
Incl: the patient has an international normalized ratio (inr) or prothrombin time (pt) of no more than 1.5 times uln unless receiving anticoagulant therapy**descriptive
Incl: the patient, if male with a pregnant partner, agrees to use a condom; no additional method of contraception is required for the pregnant partner**descriptive
Excl: the patient is unable or unwilling to take folic acid or vitamin b12 supplementation**descriptive
Incl: the patient has voluntarily agreed to participate by giving written informed consent or assent for the trial**descriptive
Excl: the patient has had an allogeneic tissue or solid organ transplant**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a hemoglobin level of at least 9 g/dl or at least 5.6 mmol/l without transfusions within 2 weeks of first dose**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a total bilirubin level less than or equal to the upper limit of normal (uln)**descriptive

Incl: the patient has a creatinine level less than or equal to 1.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln) or a calculated creatinine clearance (crcl) of at least 60 ml/min for subjects with creatinine levels greater than 1.5 times institutional uln**descriptive
Incl: the patient has an alkaline phosphatase level less than or equal to 2.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln)**descriptive
Incl: the patient has adequate organ function as indicated by laboratory values**descriptive

Incl: the patient has an international normalized ratio (inr) or prothrombin time (pt) less than or equal to 1.5 times the upper limit of normal (uln) unless receiving anticoagulant therapy**descriptive
Incl: min absolute neutrophil count (/mcl)**1500.0

Incl: the patient has no history of prior malignancy, except for basal cell carcinoma of the skin, superficial bladder cancer, squamous cell carcinoma of the skin, in situ cervical cancer, or has undergone potentially curative therapy with no evidence of disease recurrence for 5 years since initiation of that therapy**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a thyroid stimulating hormone (tsh) level within normal limits, or if not, t3 and free t4 within normal limits**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have an egfr sensitizing (activating) mutation**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received prior systemic cytotoxic chemotherapy, biological therapy, or major surgery within 3 weeks of the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Incl: min platelet count (/mcl)**100000.0
Excl: the patient has an active infection requiring intravenous systemic therapy**descriptive
Incl: the patient does not have an alk translocation**descriptive
Incl: the patient has not received prior systemic chemotherapy treatment for metastatic nsclc**descriptive

Excl: the patient has known active hepatitis c, defined by a known positive hepatitis c antibody result and known quantitative hcv rna results greater than the lower limits of detection of the assay**descriptive
Excl: the patient has interstitial lung disease (ild) or has a history of pneumonitis that has required oral or intravenous steroids**descriptive
Excl: the patient has active autoimmune disease that has required systemic treatment in the past 2 years**descriptive
Excl: the patient has a known history of human immunodeficiency virus (hiv) (hiv 1/2 antibodies)**descriptive
Excl: the patient has completed chemotherapy and/or radiation as part of neoadjuvant or adjuvant therapy at least 6 months prior to the diagnosis of metastatic disease**descriptive
Excl: the patient has known active hepatitis b, defined as a known positive hbsag result**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received thoracic radiation therapy of more than 30 gy within 6 months of the first dose of trial treatment**descriptive
Incl: the patient has measurable disease based on recist 1.1 as determined by the site**descriptive

Excl: the patient is expected to require any other form of systemic or localized antineoplastic therapy while on trial, including maintenance therapy with another agent for nsclc, radiation therapy, or surgical resection**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a pd l1 strong tumor as determined by immunohistochemistry (ihc) at a central laboratory**descriptive
Incl: the female patient of childbearing potential has a negative urine or serum pregnancy test at screening (within 72 hours of first dose of study medication)**descriptive

Incl: the female patient is of non childbearing potential, defined as at least 45 years of age and has not had menses for greater than 1 year, or is amenorrheic for less than 2 years without a hysterectomy and oophorectomy and has an fsh value in the postmenopausal range upon pretrial evaluation, or is post hysterectomy, oophorectomy, or tubal ligation as confirmed by medical records or ultrasound**descriptive
Excl: the patient has received systemic therapy for the treatment of stage iv non small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive
Excl: the patient is receiving systemic steroid therapy less than 3 days prior to the first dose of trial treatment or is receiving any other form of immunosuppressive medication**descriptive

Excl: the patient is, at the time of signing informed consent, a regular user (including recreational use) of any illicit drugs or has a recent history (within the last year) of substance abuse, including alcohol**descriptive

Excl: the patient has received prior therapy with an anti pd 1, anti pd l1, anti pd l2, anti cd137, or anti ctla 4 antibody, including ipilimumab or any other antibody or drug specifically targeting t cell co stimulation or checkpoint pathways**descriptive
Excl: the patient has untreated central nervous system (cns) metastases and/or carcinomatous meningitis identified on baseline brain imaging or prior to signing informed consent**descriptive
Incl: the patient has stage iv nsclc**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a histologically or cytologically confirmed diagnosis of non small cell lung cancer (nsclc)**descriptive
Incl: the patient has a performance status of 0 or 1 on the eastern cooperative oncology group (ecog) performance status**descriptive
Incl: the patient has serum creatinine less than or equal to 1.5 times uln**descriptive

Excl: the patient has been diagnosed with another primary malignancy other than nsclc, except for adequately treated non melanoma skin cancer or cervical cancer in situ, definitively treated non metastatic prostate cancer, or another primary malignancy that is definitively relapse free with at least 3 years elapsed since diagnosis**descriptive
